Article.ArticleTitle: Comment on Bai et al. Effect of Eccentric Training with Different Durations, Intensities, and Contraction Velocities on Upper Limb Muscle Strength: A Meta-Analysis. Life 2025, 15, 456.

Abstract.AbstractText: We read the article "Effect of Eccentric Training with Different Durations, Intensities, and Contraction Velocities on Upper Limb Muscle Strength: A Meta-Analysis" by Bai and colleagues [...].

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Neuro-Musculoskeletal Functioning and Mobility, Swiss Paraplegic Research, 6207 Nottwil, Switzerland.
Functioning Information Reference Lab, Swiss Paraplegic Research, 6207 Nottwil, Switzerland.
Neuro-Musculoskeletal Functioning and Mobility, Swiss Paraplegic Research, 6207 Nottwil, Switzerland.
Faculty of Health Sciences and Medicine, University of Lucerne, 6002 Lucerne, Switzerland.
Institute of Sports Medicine, Swiss Paraplegic Centre, 6207 Nottwil, Switzerland.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: The authors declare no conflict of interest.

Reference.Citation: Bai Z., Zhang D., Liang D., Chen X., Shi X., Chen S. Effect of Eccentric Training with Different Durations, Intensities, and Contraction Velocities on Upper Limb Muscle Strength: A Meta-Analysis. Life. 2025;15:456. doi: 10.3390/life15030456.
